name: Hedmark (Norway, 1987–2019)

colors.symbolism: the forests and agricultural land of Hedmark

description: A green field charged with three silver birch trees in a row.
history: Granted to Hedmark county on 17 April 1987. The county was abolished on 1 January 2020 when it was merged with Oppland into the new Innlandet county.

features.symbolism: heraldic banner of the county arms: three silver birch trees on a green field, evoking Hedmark's forests
the birch (Betula) is characteristic of Hedmark; three trees of the same species are a heraldic device for the county's woodlands
